questions.question: What is the complete 6-step CBT process for managing anxiety?

options.text: Breathe, Think, Feel, Act, Reflect, Repeat
Notice, Identify the thought, Name the trap, Investigate, Create a balanced thought, Notice the shift
Stop, Drop, Roll, Breathe, Count, Relax
Accept, Avoid, Adapt, Adjust, Advance, Achieve

questions.explanation: The complete CBT process is: Notice and Pause, Identify the Thought, Name the Trap, Investigate with Three Questions, Create a Balanced Thought, and Notice the Shift in your emotions.

questions.question: For a quick anxious thought in the moment, which tool is recommended?

options.text: A full 5-step Thought Record
Name the trap plus one detective question
A 30-minute meditation
Reviewing all past thought records

questions.explanation: For a quick anxious thought in the moment, the recommended approach is to name the thinking trap and apply one detective question. Full thought records are better for significant anxious episodes.

questions.question: What does the daily evening check-in involve?

options.text: Reviewing all emails from the day
Asking: What was my most anxious moment? What thought drove it? Which trap was at work? What would a balanced thought sound like?
Writing a full journal entry about every event
Practicing progressive muscle relaxation

questions.explanation: The 5-minute evening check-in involves four questions: What was my most anxious moment today? What thought drove it? Which trap was at work? What would a balanced thought sound like? Over time, this becomes second nature.

questions.question: What makes the combination of cognitive tools and body-based strategies particularly effective?

options.text: They cancel each other out
The combination of mind-based and body-based strategies is what makes lasting change possible
Body strategies work better alone
Cognitive tools are only effective with medication

questions.explanation: The combination of mind-based strategies (thought records, thinking traps, detective questions) and body-based strategies (breathing, grounding, relaxation) creates a comprehensive approach that makes lasting change possible.

questions.question: What five cognitive skills have you built in Section 2 of the Anxiety Toolkit?

options.text: Breathing, grounding, TIPP, exposure, and relaxation
Recognizing the CBT cycle, identifying thinking traps, three detective questions, creating balanced thoughts, and using thought records
Meditation, yoga, journaling, gratitude, and self-compassion
Diagnosis, treatment, medication, therapy, and prevention

questions.explanation: The five cognitive skills are: recognizing the Thought-Feeling-Action cycle, identifying Thinking Traps, using three detective questions, creating balanced thoughts, and using Thought Records to track and transform thinking.

title: Putting CBT Tools Together: Building Mental Clarity - Quiz
